column19: Inter-disciplinary collaboration; to understand the computational resources of brains and to build linking principles from brain to behavior using computational models. This goal is being pursued with a combination of theoretical and experimental approaches at several levels of investigation ranging from the biophysical level to the systems level. Hippocampal and cortical slice preparations are being used to explore the properties of single neurons and synapses, including the precision of spike firing and the influence of neuromodulators. Biophysical models of electrical and chemical signal processing within neurons are used as an adjunct to physiological experiments. New techniques have been developed for modeling cell signaling using Monte Carlo methods (MCell); how dendrites integrate synaptic signals in neurons; how networks of neurons generate dynamical patterns of activity; what regulates the flow of information into the cerebral cortex and between cortical areas; how memory representations are formed and consolidated during sleep; and how visuo-motor transformations are adaptively organized; analyzing the sources for electrical and magnetic signals recorded from the scalp and hemodynamic signals from functional neuroimaging by blind separation using ICA; AI; recording the electrical activity of select sets of cells, as well as analyzes thin slices of autopsied brains, using that information to create and refine computational models on how the brain stores information for different activities and, through these models, getting a better understanding of what information different cell types encode, what molecules are needed and how signals move throughout the brain. At the same time, learning how diseases such as schizophrenia or Parkinson’s might alter these patterns; linking brain mechanisms to behaviour; and sleep

column22:
I recently got furloughed a few weeks before relocating to London to join the core team at How to Change the World, where I was working as a Program Manager and Learning Experience Designer. In that role, I built UN SDG-based, project-based virtual bootcamps for global universities and managed key partnerships.
 
 While the furlough was officially for three months, it happened because Google Next absorbed my learning experience design responsibilities. Combined with how the furlough was handled, this led me to lose trust in the organization.
 
 Though I had a fantastic experience with my immediate team, I’m now looking to connect with values-aligned people and opportunities where integrity and follow-through are central to the work.
